title: Dactylorchis longifolia
intro: Dactylorchis longifolia, also known as the Spiranthes autumnalis or the autumn lady's tresses, is a type of terrestrial orchid that grows in temperate regions. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Dactylorchis longifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to relieve respiratory symptoms and support overall well-being.
taxonomy: Dactylorchis longifolia is a species within the genus Dactylorchis and the family Orchidaceae. It is a type of terrestrial orchid that belongs to the family of flowering plants known as orchids. The species Dactylorchis longifolia is further classified within the subfamily Orchidoideae. The classification of this orchid species is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular analysis. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and diversity within the orchid family.
benefits: Dactylorchis longifolia, also known as the spotted orchid or lady's slipper, has been studied for its potential health benefits.
